intro: Raddiella luetzelburgii is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, commonly known as "Tetilla de Vaca" or "Cow's Udder," due to its unique, udder-like shape. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in traditional medicine for treating various ailments, including wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. Raddiella luetzelburgii contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Raddiella luetzelburgii is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, often used to treat fever, cough, and skin conditions, as well as to promote wound healing.
